sections.label: Offset / Production Cap
sections.value: PPA / Loan (all utilities): cannot exceed 103% offset. Cash (all utilities): prefer 100% actual production; 103% is the absolute max allowed.

sections.label: Usage & Production
sections.value: Design to the customer's historical usage PLUS the legislated CT adders (EV / Heat Pump, below). Do not cap production at usage -- the old 'do not exceed usage amount with production' guideline is incorrect for CT. 5-year lookback (new homeowner on an existing home): at Interconnection review the utility approves the highest 12-month usage from the past 5 years, plus adders. That history is not available at point of sale; if the rep can obtain the prior bills from the customer, submit them so the larger system can be honored.

sections.label: Max System Size
sections.value: 25kW-AC (inverter) is the max system allowed in CT. If the original system is part of the RRES program (installed 2022 or later), the MAX AC for both systems combined cannot exceed 25kW (inverter size).

value.sunlight: 2.050kW - EPC cap based on credit score

sections.label: Load Calc & Usage Rules
sections.value: State does NOT allow a load calc. It may be used for internal purposes only -- the utility will not accept it. The utility has final say and can reject a system for being oversized if it was sized based on a load calc.

sections.label: Inverter Sizing
sections.value: 9 kW AC inverter cap: applies specifically to NEW BUILDS and homes with NO usage history (this is the scenario, not a blanket CT rule). Build to the available usage and do not exceed the 9 kW AC inverter cap. Operational note: no stocked inverter combination totals exactly 9 kW, so these designs require Micro Inverters (see the Special Work / Micro Inverter SOP). CT allows 28 (410-watt) panels on a SE7.6K inverter.

sections.label: Electric Vehicle
sections.value: Legislated adder: up to two electric vehicles, +3,285 kWh each, added on top of the customer's historical usage (max 2, regardless of how many the customer plans to buy). Documentation (all purchase methods): sign the CT MOU and indicate the adder in the combined packet -- proof of purchase / receipts are no longer required. EV or Heat Pump with less than 12 months of history: design over the 9 kW AC cap by quoting the 9 kW system first, then adding the allowed adder production.

sections.label: Heat Pump
sections.value: Legislated adder on top of the customer's historical usage: whole-home Air Source Heat Pump +3,608 kWh; whole-home Ground Source Heat Pump +2,458 kWh. Documentation (all purchase methods): sign the CT MOU and indicate the adder in the combined packet.

sections.label: Ground Mount
sections.value: Minimum panel count of 15, Net PPW $5.00 after trenching, and a multiplier of 1.2 or higher.

sections.label: Roof Pitch
sections.value: CT allows up to 52 degrees pitch without approval. IGS pitch limits: 10-45 degrees.

sections.label: Detached Structure
sections.value: A minimum of six 410-watt panels allowed on a detached structure. Back feeding for detached structures is never an option. Trenching must always be accounted for when a detached structure is designed on.

sections.label: Add-On Systems
sections.value: Eversource requires 12 months of bills to calculate the Annual Usage post-PTO. UI requires 1 bill (the graph is accurate for usage).

sections.label: Tree Removal Vendors
sections.value: Trinity has tree vendors in CT if the rep requests Trinity to remove trees.

sections.label: AHJ Confirmation
sections.value: Based on the Township listed on the Title. Does not require an external website to confirm accuracy.

sections.label: Setbacks
sections.value: State-wide setbacks must be followed.

sections.label: Cash Contract Requirements
sections.value: All Cash Contracts require the sales rep's HIS License Number, located in Salesforce.

sections.label: Notes
sections.value: Check the Contact List in SharePoint for local office contacts.

sections.label: Offset / Production Cap
sections.value: All Utilities: cannot exceed 110% actual production. State allows up to 200% if the deal is signed and we are one panel over 110% actual production -- push it forward without a change order.

value.sunlight: 2.050kW - EPC cap based on credit score

sections.label: Load Calc & Usage Rules
sections.value: Less than 6 months on the bill: load calc required. 6-11 months: average for the missing months and build to this; if the rep provided a load calc, build to the load calc usage. Required if under 6 months, can be used if 6-11 months. Required along with a Letter of Explanation if the rep is requesting over 110%.

sections.label: Electric Vehicle
sections.value: No information currently available. If a deal comes up, reach out to a manager.

sections.label: Ground Mount
sections.value: Minimum panel count of 15, minimum Net PPW $4.00 after non-DC and trenching. Ballasted systems follow the same pricing.

sections.value: Roofs with a pitch of 46-49 degrees need local office approval. IGS pitch limits: 10-45 degrees.

sections.label: Detached Structure
sections.value: A minimum of six 410-watt panels allowed on a detached structure.

sections.value: Baltimore Gas & Electric, Delmarva Power, and PEPCO need 1 bill (graph is accurate for after-solar usage).

sections.value: To confirm the AHJ, visit the Maryland Taxation website. Most townships have a version of the IRC setback code implemented.

sections.label: Offset / Production Cap
sections.value: By utility -- JCP&L: cannot exceed 100% PVWatts (Utility Production in Genesis). PSE&G: cannot exceed 100% PVWatts (Utility Production in Genesis). Vineland: cannot exceed 90% actual production. Atlantic City Electric (ACE): cannot exceed the max inverter size based on the inverter equation Annual Usage / 1.2 (e.g., 8,345 / 1.2 = 6,954, so max inverter is a 6.0K). Most feeders have a 5.0kW-AC inverter restriction; check the Atlantic City Electric map to confirm the circuit is open.

value.sunlight: 2.050kW - EPC cap based on credit score

sections.label: Load Calc & Usage Rules
sections.value: Less than 12 months on the bill: load calc required. We CANNOT average for missing months in any utility. If there are 12 months and the rep is adamant about needing additional usage, chatter the @NJ Applications handle with the Utility Company, System Size, and Offset; ask if they foresee issues with the utility approving the oversize and whether additional info (e.g., LOE) is required; CC the rep. Only proceed with the official LC/LOE request if NJ Applications believes the utility will approve. Note in Salesforce: 'System Size is contingent upon the utility company approving applications - Initials'. PSE&G: does not accept a load calc for customers with 12 months usage without full, dated supporting documents -- rep must provide receipts and proof of any work done with the LC and LOE. PSE&G may still reject the oversize.

sections.label: Electric Vehicle
sections.value: JCP&L, ACE, and PSE&G need proof of purchase; if it's a Tesla that was delivered, need the delivery receipt, load calc, and letter of explanation. PSE&G additionally needs car registration showing the address matches the PSE&G account address, plus proof of purchase of an EV charger. Based on the 'NJ EV Info' form from 6.25.24.

sections.label: Detached Structure

sections.value: Atlantic City Electric (ACE), PSE&G, and Vineland need 1 bill (graph is accurate for after-solar usage). JCP&L requires 12 bills or usage history showing the 'kWh Billed' amount.

sections.label: Rebates
sections.value: Orange & Rockland and Central Hudson rebate = $0.15/watt; if the average is below 70%, multiply by the efficiency (reference the math guide). PSEG-LI, Con-Ed, and NYSEG do NOT have rebates. NYSERDA rebates have a max system size of 25.000kW (if the system is larger than 25kW, the rebate is calculated using 25kW).

sections.label: Load Calc & Usage Rules
sections.value: Less than 12 months on the bill: load calc required (see Duquesne Light and PPL below). Required along with a Letter of Explanation if requesting over 110%. Duquesne Light: load calc required if the customer has less than 12 months on the bill and we are building above the usage history shown -- you can build to the usage on the bill or to a load calc. PPL: if less than 12 months, build to the usage on the bill or to the load calc the rep provided; PPL has final approval and can reject the use of the load calc.

sections.label: Electric Vehicle
sections.value: Customer will need to sign an MOU for the overproduction.

sections.label: Ground Mount
sections.value: Minimum panel count of 15, minimum Net PPW $4.10 after non-DC and trenching. Dan Riddle must approve.

sections.label: Car Port
sections.value: Only allowed in PA. Minimum Net PPW $4.50.

sections.label: Roof Orientation
sections.value: Cannot design on roofs with an orientation between 290-70 degrees (northern roofs) unless the rep asks to max all roofs or include northern roofs. The minimum system size for the City of Pittsburgh is 5.0kW (thirteen 410-watt panels) when the AHJ is City of Pittsburgh; if the AHJ is anything else, 5 panels is the minimum system size.

sections.label: Roof Pitch
sections.value: Roofs with a pitch of 46-49 degrees need local office approval. IGS pitch limits: 10-45 degrees.

sections.label: Detached Structure
sections.value: A minimum of six 410-watt panels allowed on a detached structure.

sections.label: Net Metering (Schedule Z)
sections.value: PA allows Schedule Z (virtual net metering); each utility has different rules. Chatter Mike Davis on each opportunity with specific questions if needed.

sections.label: Add-On Systems
sections.value: Met-Ed requires 12 bills. PPL needs 1 bill (the graph is accurate). PECO requires 1 bill (graph is accurate). DLC requires 1 bill (graph is accurate; bars that go below 0 should be entered as 0). Other utilities are TBD.

sections.label: Tree Removal Vendors
sections.value: Trinity has tree vendors in PA if the rep requests Trinity to remove trees.

sections.value: AHJ must be confirmed on the Pennsylvania Flood Map website, or if the title shows Dauphin County, use the Dauphin County website.
